ledeAttribution.prefix: Collision-room practice adapted from work by
ledeAttribution.suffix: — who built the Pacific desk and proved the model works across a continent.

practiceGrid.heading: Collision room
practiceGrid.body: A named group of distinct AI voices, each with a defined register and a genuine point of view. Not a panel of assistants — a writer’s room. The desk takes what the room produces.

practiceGrid.heading: Voice, not chatbot
practiceGrid.body: Each member has a specific character — their idiom, their preoccupations, their blind spots. Borges doesn’t speak like Stringer Bell. That’s the point.

practiceGrid.heading: The desk writes
practiceGrid.body: The rooms are read-only. They propose, push back, surface. The human at the desk writes the actual response, citing whose angle they took.

practiceGrid.heading: The room compounds
practiceGrid.body: Different minds with different gifts, in the same room at the same time, produce something none of them would have alone. Agree, disagree, ramble, cut each other off — something comes out the other side. That’s the whole bet.
